records.text: Soft tissue swelling noted around joint; rule out sprain/strain vs. septic arthritis.
Hypertrophic osteodystrophy (HOD) suspected; radiographs show characteristic 'double physeal line'. Supportive care/pain management.
My dog's kneecap pops in and out of place (luxating patella).
Cat is less playful than usual.
Immune-mediated polyarthritis suspected; perform joint taps on multiple joints (carpi, stifles, hocks), ANA titer, rheumatoid factor.
Chronic arthritis; start laser therapy.
Radiographs confirm moderate to severe hip dysplasia with secondary OA changes.
Osteochondritis dissecans (OCD) flap identified on caudal aspect of humeral head.
Guinea pig has swollen feet (bumblefoot).
Recommend physical therapy/rehabilitation (e.g., underwater treadmill, laser therapy, passive range of motion exercises).
My dog injured his back (IVDD) and requires strict rest.
My dog sometimes skips for a step or two on his back leg while trotting.
Dog’s gait is wobbly and he falls often.
Polymyositis suspected; check CK levels, consider muscle biopsy.
My ferret seems painful in his back.
CSF tap performed; analysis pending.
My dog pants heavily even when resting indoors.
Cat has difficulty navigating stairs.
My older dog seems painful and slow when getting up after naps.
Tibial compression test positive.
Radiographs show mixed lytic/proliferative lesion in proximal humerus. Differentials include primary bone tumor (OSA) vs. metastatic condition vs. fungal osteomyelitis.
My dog has elbow dysplasia.
Ununited anconeal process (UAP) identified on elbow radiographs.
Schedule recheck appointment in 1-2 weeks to assess initial response to pain medication.
Recommend rehabilitation therapy including hydrotherapy and therapeutic exercises.
Lameness with ataxia; test for spinal lesion.
Suspect supraspinatus tendinopathy; diagnostic musculoskeletal ultrasound recommended.
My Doberman has Wobbler's, causing a clumsy gait.
Dog has difficulty posturing to urinate or defecate.
Cat meows loudly if I accidentally brush against her lower back.
Rabbit has E. cuniculi infection causing neurological/mobility issues.
Puppy hops with both back legs together like a bunny when running.
Cat has started pooping outside the litter box on the rug.
Consider therapeutic laser sessions.
Cat has arthritis secondary to previous injury.
Dog trembles sometimes, especially his back legs, when standing.
Provide structured plan for gradual increase in activity following healing period.
Caudal hip luxation identified; attempt closed reduction under anesthesia.
Myelogram reveals extradural spinal cord compression at T13-L1.
My dog's back legs seem to cross over when he walks.
Characteristic gait abnormality and muscle atrophy consistent with infraspinatus contracture.
My dog sits down awkwardly ('puppy sitting') with one leg out.
Perform baseline CBC/Chemistry panel before starting NSAIDs; recheck periodically.
Positive response to Tensilon (edrophonium chloride) injection supports diagnosis of myasthenia gravis.
Cat suddenly lost use of her back legs, they feel cold.
Reassess pain control at each visit.
Disc herniation; prescribe strict rest and steroids.
Myelogram performed, shows spinal cord compression.
Rabbit has developed urine scald from inability to posture correctly.
My dog cries when getting up or lying down.
My dog has panosteitis (growing pains).
Referral to neurologist for MRI and potential surgical decompression for IVDD.
Rabbit has a broken leg after an accident.
Calculate RER and adjust caloric intake to achieve/maintain ideal body weight.
Prescribe strict cage rest (4-6 weeks) and muscle relaxants (methocarbamol) for suspected fibrocartilaginous embolism (FCE).
Radiographs show L7-S1 spondylosis and mild narrowing of neural foramen.
Referral for neurological consultation/advanced imaging (MRI/CT) recommended for suspected IVDD/spinal cord condition.
Provide estimate for diagnostics and treatment options.
Administer gabapentin or amantadine for neuropathic pain/chronic OA pain.
My dog has developed a head tilt.
My dog has trouble balancing.
Ferret is weak in the hind limbs.
Cat moves with a stiff, hunched posture.
Recommend joint supplements containing glucosamine, chondroitin, omega-3 fatty acids.
My dog has myasthenia gravis causing weakness.
Suspect vitamin C deficiency (scurvy) in guinea pig with lameness/reluctance to move; supplement vitamin C.
My dog stumbles frequently.
Proprioceptive training exercises recommended.
Carpal hyperextension injury.
Initiate multimodal pain management: NSAID (e.g., firocoxib) + gabapentin.
My dog has arthritis diagnosed by the vet.
Splint or cast applied for stabilization.
My dog favors one leg consistently.
Cat has painful swelling around multiple joints.
My dog has osteosarcoma (bone cancer).
My cat used to sleep on the top bunk, now she stays on the floor.
Positive cranial drawer sign in right stifle; consistent with cranial cruciate ligament (CCL) rupture.
Trial of amantadine added to NSAID/gabapentin for refractory OA pain (NMDA receptor antagonist).
Marked crepitus and decreased range of motion in left stifle; suspect severe OA.
Paraspinal hyperesthesia noted in T13-L2 region. Rule out Type I IVDD.
My dog's hind legs seem uncoordinated, crossing over each other.
Cat has hypertrophic osteodystrophy.
Infraspinatus contracture noted.
Discuss prognosis based on diagnosis.
Palpation elicits pain response over carpus/elbow/shoulder (specify joint).
My dog has intervertebral disc condition (IVDD).
Cat is overgrooming areas near painful joints.
My dog takes very short, stiff steps with his front legs.
Ununited anconeal process (UAP) visible on flexed lateral elbow radiograph.
My dog's gait looks wobbly or uncoordinated.
Rule out non-musculoskeletal causes of reluctance to move (e.g., systemic illness, abdominal pain).
Cat isn't grooming her hindquarters well, fur looks matted.
Teach owner passive range of motion (PROM) exercises and massage techniques.
Orthopedic exam components: observation, palpation of bones/joints/muscles, range of motion, instability tests (drawer, Ortolani).
Cat has decreased appetite, possibly due to chronic pain.
My ferret arches his back when I pick him up.
Monitor for cast sores or swelling distal to bandage.
Spinal palpation elicits pain in thoracolumbar region; suspect intervertebral disc condition (IVDD).
Monitor renal and hepatic function in patients on long-term NSAIDs.
Cat has a poor appetite, which might be due to chronic joint pain.
My dog's hips make a popping sound when he walks.
My dog yelps when jumping off the couch.
My dog has Legg-Calve-Perthes condition.
Rabbit has lost bladder/bowel control.
My dog's back seems hunched or arched.
Dog tires easily on walks and wants to turn back early.
Cat likely has a muscle sprain or strain causing limping.
Pain elicited on extension of bilateral hips and palpation of lumbar spine.
Severe ulcerative pododermatitis (bumblefoot) in guinea pig; debride, bandage, systemic antibiotics, improve husbandry.
Rabbit has urine staining on its hind legs from poor posture.
Recommend installing ramps, using non-slip mats, providing orthopedic bedding, and using raised food/water bowls.
My dog doesn't lift his legs as high when walking anymore.
Cutaneous trunci reflex cutoff identified.
Radiographs show bilateral stifle effusion.
Discuss Adequan (polysulfated glycosaminoglycan) injections for OA management.
Rule out food allergy as cause of non-seasonal pruritus.
Rule out hyperadrenocorticism via ACTH stimulation test or LDDST.
Erythematous patches on muzzle; rule out autoimmune issue.
Lesions consistent with mosquito bite hypersensitivity.
Dog is rubbing his face on the carpet constantly.
Flea bite hypersensitivity is primary differential for caudal dorsal pruritus.
Dog has intense itching primarily at night.
Dog’s ears have a rash with small, crusty bumps.
Perianal irritation and scooting; express anal sacs, check for tapeworms.
Dog has redness and bumps in the groin area.
Poor coat quality, excessive scaling (seborrhea sicca); evaluate diet, check thyroid.
Chronic otitis externa and recurrent skin infections; strong suspicion for underlying atopic dermatitis.
My rabbit is biting at its flank.
Pododermatitis with interdigital swelling and discharge; cytology shows yeast and bacteria.
Multiple comedones present; suggests endocrine or keratinization defect.
Lichenification and hyperpigmentation in axillary regions; chronic allergic skin condition likely.
Excoriations on tail; rule out flea hypersensitivity.
Crusting lesions on pinnal margins; suspect sarcoptic mange or vasculitis.
Pododermatitis in guinea pig; assess husbandry (substrate, cleanliness), rule out infection/scurvy.
Immune-mediated skin condition suspected; requires further workup.
Papular rash on legs; antihistamines recommended.
Dog has oily, smelly skin, especially along the back.
Noticed a small, wart-like growth on my dog's eyelid.
Multiple lipomas palpated in subcutaneous tissue; monitor size and number.
My hamster has developed a lump under its skin.
Ferret is scratching under its arms.
Skin fragility noted; test for FIV/FeLV in feline patient.
Cat has scaly skin on her back.
My puppy has developed a swollen muzzle and face.
My rabbit has matted fur and sores underneath on its hindquarters.
Differentials include bacterial folliculitis, demodicosis, dermatophytosis.
Trichogram reveals damaged hair shafts consistent with excessive licking.
Fungal culture positive for Microsporum canis; initiate systemic antifungal (itraconazole) and topical therapy.
Skin biopsy submitted for histopathology and culture.
My cat is sensitive to being petted along her back.
My hamster is losing fur around its eyes.
Feline patient exhibits miliary dermatitis pattern; rule out FAD, food allergy, atopy.
My guinea pig has bald spots appearing behind its ears.
My kitten has lost the fur on the bridge of her nose.
Post-clipping alopecia noted on flank; advise owner regrowth may be slow or incomplete.
Cat has scaly patches on her ear tips.
My dog has a rash after swimming in the lake.
My dog's nose looks crusty and has lost its pigment.
My dog has persistent itching despite flea treatment.
My dog has developed skin tags all over his body.
My rabbit is scratching its dewlap constantly.
Recommend omega-3 fatty acid supplementation to support skin barrier.
Juvenile cellulitis ('puppy strangles') diagnosed; initiate immunosuppressive steroid therapy.
Alopecia, scaling, and follicular casts present; consistent with sebaceous adenitis. Biopsy recommended.
Cytology from interdigital space reveals numerous Malassezia organisms and neutrophils.
Educate owner on recognizing early signs of skin infection.
Scaling and redness on back; medicated bath advised.
Post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation present in previously inflamed areas.
Cat is excessively grooming her front legs.
Sebaceous adenitis suspected due to follicular casts and alopecia; biopsy confirms.
My dog has developed a large fluid-filled swelling (cyst?) under the skin.
Papules and erythema on chest; medicated wash prescribed.
My ferret's skin looks yellowish and he's losing fur.
Seborrhea with erythema on limbs; shampoo therapy started.
Eosinophilic granuloma complex lesion on lip; biopsy recommended.
Histiocytoma observed on young dog's limb; typically benign, monitor for regression.
Check FeLV/FIV status in cats with unusual or severe skin condition.
Post-clipping alopecia noted; reassure owner hair regrowth may be slow.
Culture and sensitivity results: Staphylococcus pseudintermedius sensitive to cephalexin.
Color dilution alopecia suspected in blue-coated dog with poor coat quality and folliculitis.
My hamster seems to have greasy fur and is losing hair on its back.
Excoriations around ears and neck; check for ear mites and fleas.
My cat has bald patches above her eyes.
Prescribe short course of prednisone for severe inflammation.
Consider environmental allergy testing (serum or intradermal).
Recommend regular ear cleaning with appropriate solution.
Noticed thickened, dark skin in my dog's armpits that seems itchy.
Wood's lamp examination negative for Microsporum canis fluorescence.
Urticarial reaction observed; administer diphenhydramine and monitor.
Use E-collar to prevent self-trauma.
Measure and record size of any skin masses.
Dog has black spots appearing on his skin, mostly on the belly.
Juvenile cellulitis (puppy strangles) presenting with facial swelling and pustules.
Hygroma noted on elbow; advise padded bedding, monitor for infection.
My dog has developed a lump that seems attached to the skin.
Myiasis present in neglected wound; clean, debride, remove larvae.
Nasal hyperkeratosis noted; advise emollients/keratolytic agents.
Discuss potential side effects of long-term steroid use.
My puppy has what looks like acne on his belly.
Disseminated histoplasmosis can cause chronic large bowel diarrhea and weight loss; check urine antigen test.
Dog has lost his appetite and seems nauseous (licking lips, drooling).
Assess hydration status (skin turgor, mucous membranes, CRT).
Noticed bright red blood streaks in my cat's poop.
Cobalamin (B12) levels low; initiate supplementation.
Dietary modification: increase fiber for constipation/colitis.
My dog is eating grass obsessively and then vomiting foam.
Campylobacter jejuni isolated from fecal culture; treat with erythromycin if clinical signs warrant.
Severe dental tartar and gingivitis noted; may contribute to hyporexia or dysphagia.
My rabbit vomited a small amount of green fluid.
My rabbit's appetite has been poor for several days.
My senior cat hasn't pooped in four days and seems uncomfortable.
Perineal hernia noted; contributes to tenesmus and constipation. Surgical repair advised.
My cat is regurgitating food (undigested, tube-shaped) right after eating.
Feline panleukopenia suspected in unvaccinated kitten with severe V/D.
Monitor urine output (>1-2 ml/kg/hr) as indicator of adequate perfusion and hydration.
Acute vomiting and diarrhea; suspect gastroenteritis.
Endoscopy with biopsies recommended for definitive diagnosis of IBD/lymphoma.
Dog has yellowish diarrhea.
My puppy ate a sock yesterday and is now vomiting.
Cat vomited what looks like coffee grounds.
Instruct owner on proper administration of oral medications.
My dog's stomach is rumbling loudly, and he keeps licking the air.
Puppy has on-and-off diarrhea since we brought him home.
Guinea pig has stopped producing fecal pellets.
Radiographs show diffuse gas pattern throughout bowel; no signs of obstruction.
My rabbit is producing 'string of pearls' feces (hair linked together).
Advise slow transition when changing pet foods.
Hamster is not eating or drinking and looks hunched over.
Dog ate something from the trash and now has explosive diarrhea.
Congenital portosystemic shunt suspected in young dog with poor growth, neuro signs after eating, urate crystalluria.
Puppy vomited his dinner whole, hours after eating.
Consider empirical deworming with broad-spectrum anthelmintic.
Elevated ALT and ALP; consider primary hepatopathy vs. reactive hepatopathy. Bile acids test recommended.
My hamster's droppings are strung together with hair.
My puppy seems to have a sensitive stomach, gets diarrhea easily.
Auscultation reveals hyperactive borborygmi.
My cat vomited, then had diarrhea, now just hiding.
My dog just seems 'off' - lethargic, poor appetite.
Nasogastric tube placed for nutritional support.
Esophagostomy tube placed for long-term nutritional support in anorexic feline patient.
My cat only poops every 3-4 days, and the stool is very hard.
Recommend feeding smaller, more frequent meals.
My cat has intermittent constipation, goes several days without defecating.
Cat seems painful in her abdomen when I pick her up.
Puppy has intermittent diarrhea, sometimes normal, sometimes loose.
My dog has extremely foul-smelling gas.
My rabbit has a dirty bottom from soft, pasty stools.
Cat is losing weight despite having a good appetite.
Large bowel diarrhea suspected based on mucus and hematochezia.
My ferret is lethargic and passing dark, sticky feces.
Fecal analysis: Direct smear negative for motile organisms, flotation negative for ova/parasites.
Large bowel diarrhea characterized by increased frequency, tenesmus, mucus, and hematochezia.
Stool culture pending; start supportive care.
My rabbit is stretching out frequently, looks uncomfortable.
My cat vomited a worm.
Cat vomited something that looked dark brown, like old blood.
My dog seems to have heartburn (gulping, licking air).
Supportive care for pancreatitis: IV fluids, pain management (buprenorphine), anti-emetics, NPO initially.
Dog has diarrhea that looks like raspberry jam.
Fecal PCR for Tritrichomonas foetus recommended for refractory feline diarrhea.
Gut dysbiosis likely; recommend synbiotic (probiotic + prebiotic).
My cat vomited part of a houseplant leaf.
Hemorrhagic gastroenteritis (HGE): PCV 68%, TP 7.5 g/dL, raspberry jam stool. Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation required.
Cat is losing weight but eating more than usual.
Pain medication (buprenorphine) administered for abdominal discomfort.
My cat tries to vomit frequently but only retches.
Dog has diarrhea that smells metallic, like blood.
Guinea pig's abdomen feels tight and bloated.
Dog has black, tar-like stool.
Pet’s belly is distended and he’s lethargic.
Perform ACTH stimulation test to rule out Addison's.
Dog is retching forcefully but producing nothing.
My dog's poop is covered in slimy mucus.
Addison's condition (hypoadrenocorticism) can present with waxing/waning GI signs.
Prescribe anti-diarrheal medication (loperamide) cautiously.
Cat suddenly refuses to eat her usual food.
Guinea pig has soft, smelly droppings.
Differential diagnoses for vomiting include dietary indiscretion, FB, pancreatitis, renal condition, toxins, IBD, neoplasia.
Cat has shown no interest in food for 24 hours.
My cat is drinking excessive amounts of water and vomiting frequently.
Ultrasound reveals diffusely thickened small intestinal walls with loss of layering. Suggestive of IBD or infiltrative neoplasia (lymphoma).
Hepatic encephalopathy suspected due to neurological signs (head pressing, disorientation) and liver dysfunction (elevated bile acids).
Hamster has fecal staining and wetness around the tail and belly.
Ferret has green, slimy diarrhea.
Cat has excessive flatulence and occasional vomiting.
Vomiting post-meal; test for food intolerance.
Lymphangiectasia suspected based on ultrasound and hypoalbuminemia.
My hamster has diarrhea and a wet tail area.
Administer anti-emetic (maropitant injection) and recommend hospitalization for IV fluids and further diagnostics/surgery.
Educate owner on recognizing signs of dehydration (lethargy, sunken eyes, tacky gums).
Liver enzymes elevated; consider primary liver condition or secondary reaction.
My guinea pig's droppings are much smaller and fewer than usual.
History of dietary indiscretion (garbage ingestion).
My cat is drinking tons of water and vomited twice yesterday.
Histoplasmosis can cause chronic large bowel diarrhea.
My cat has been hiding under the bed and won't eat.
Kidney values elevated; assess hydration and rule out primary renal condition.
Chronic history of intermittent vomiting and weight loss in older feline. Differentials include IBD, lymphoma, hyperthyroidism.
Discontinue any NSAIDs that could be causing GI upset.
Discontinue potentially offending medication (e.g., new antibiotic) and monitor for resolution of GI signs.
Dog has greasy, pale, voluminous stools.
Vomiting and lethargy; check for intoxication.
Consider parasite resistance if treatment fails; switch drug class.
Ferret has black, tarry stools, could it be from parasites?
My kitten is covered in fleas, they are crawling all over.
My cockatiel is listless and has abnormal droppings.
Check FeLV/FIV status in cats with demodicosis.
Puppy is being treated for scabies (Sarcoptic mange).
My puppy has persistent diarrhea, vet found coccidia.
Prescribe isoxazoline (e.g., Bravecto, Nexgard, Simparica, Credelio) for flea/tick control.
Dog has severe flea infestation, requiring immediate intervention.
Rule out Taenia vs. Dipylidium tapeworms based on history (hunting vs. fleas).
Prescribe fluralaner (Bravecto) for 12-week flea and tick control.
My rabbit has severe crusting in ears from Psoroptes mites.
Recommend bathing pet prior to applying topical ectoparasiticides if coat is dirty.
Dog has ticks in his ears.
Recommend screening for tick-borne conditions 4-8 weeks after known tick exposure, even if asymptomatic.
Roundworm infection; start pyrantel therapy.
Flea eggs on fur; prescribe oral spinosad.
Lice infestation; apply topical permethrin.
Perform MDR1 genetic testing before using ivermectin/moxidectin in potentially sensitive breeds.
Initiate doxycycline therapy for anaplasmosis/ehrlichiosis/Lyme condition.
Intestinal parasites should be considered a differential for chronic vomiting or diarrhea.
Advise steam cleaning or dilute bleach solution for environmental Coccidia control.
Kitten failed to respond to initial dewormer, suspecting resistant strain or different parasite.
My finch is wheezing and clicking, suspect air sac mites.
Provide handout on heartworm condition.
Recommend topical flea/tick preventative (e.g., fipronil, selamectin, isoxazoline).
Found a long, flat, white worm in my dog's fresh stool.
Cat has tiny scabs all over her back, feels like sandpaper.
Trixacarus caviae (guinea pig mite) infestation; treat with ivermectin/selamectin.
Pediculosis (lice infestation) confirmed; treat with appropriate insecticide.
Provide client handout detailing comprehensive flea control strategy (pet and environment).
My ferret is itchy, especially around his neck and shoulders.
My cat has resistant ear mites.
Administer dewormer according to manufacturer's instructions.
Prescribe pyrantel pamoate for roundworm and hookworm treatment.
My canary has crusty scales on its legs and feet.
Babesia canis identified via PCR. Treatment options discussed (imidocarb dipropionate).
Recommend initiation of monthly isoxazoline product (e.g., Bravecto, Nexgard, Simparica) for flea/tick control.
Provide handout on tick prevention and removal.
Kitten has persistent diarrhea despite deworming.
Administer praziquantel injection (Droncit) for cestode infection.
My cat passed several long, white, round worms in her stool.
Educate owner on parasite life cycles and prevention strategies.
My dog requires treatment for heartworm infection.
Localized demodicosis in puppy typically resolves spontaneously; monitor closely, treat if lesions spread.
Kitten has Coccidia causing bloody diarrhea.
Initiate treatment for sarcoptic mange (e.g., selamectin, ivermectin, isoxazoline).
Use milbemycin oxime/spinosad (Trifexis) for heartworm/flea/intestinal parasite prevention (dogs only).
Fecal Giardia antigen test performed using SNAP or ELISA method.
My cat's skin irritation (miliary dermatitis) is caused by fleas.
Demodex mites detected; start oral ivermectin.
Check for concurrent infections in immunocompromised animals.
Babesia gibsoni identified on blood smear; requires specific treatment protocol.
Heartworm antigen test positive; stage condition and plan treatment.
Rabbit is shaking its head and digging at its ears.
Document specific parasite identified and treatment administered in medical record.
Heartworm antigen test negative. Microfilaria test negative.
Use imidacloprid/moxidectin (Advantage Multi) for heartworm/flea/mite/intestinal parasite control.
Rabbit's fur is matted and it seems itchy underneath.
My dog has scabies, and the whole family is itchy now.
My cat hunts mice, should I be worried about worms?
My bird has scaly mites on its legs.
My dog keeps biting at his tail base, and his hair is thinning there.
Differentiate Taenia vs. Dipylidium proglottids if possible (Dipylidium motile, Taenia rectangular).
Kitten has hookworms according to the fecal test.
Ticks embedded in fur; remove and treat.
My dog has scabies, and now I'm itchy too!
Heartworm treatment protocol initiated (doxycycline, preventative, melarsomine injections).
Trixacarus caviae infestation in guinea pig causing intense pruritus, scaling, seizures. Treat with ivermectin/selamectin.
My dog licks his front paws incessantly, vet mentioned possible Demodex.
Dog has red bumps all over his belly, and I found black 'dirt' that smears red.
Rule out aberrant parasite migration (e.g., Cuterebra in brain) with advanced imaging if neurological signs present.
Dog gets intensely itchy and breaks out in hives after just one flea bite.
Fecal smear/flotation positive for Giardia cysts/trophozoites.
Confirm pet's lifestyle (indoor/outdoor, travel history) to assess parasite risk.
Fecal examination positive for Cystoisospora (Coccidia) oocysts.
Guinea pig has Trixacarus mange, causing intense itching and seizures.
There are little white things that look like rice grains stuck to the fur around my dog's anus.
Kitten is sneezing and has discharge, could it be lungworms?
Fecal centrifugation with zinc sulfate solution used for optimal parasite egg recovery.
My puppy scoots his bottom on the floor frequently.
Dog has ticks attached deep inside his ear canals.
My rabbit has fur mites (Cheyletiella).
Rabbit has Passalurus ambiguus (pinworms).
Symptomatic treatment for pruritus (e.g., Apoquel, Cytopoint, steroids) may be needed initially.
Guinea pig has visible lice crawling on its hair shafts.
My cat vomited something that looked like a small worm.
Provide client handout explaining heartworm life cycle, transmission, and prevention.
Treat generalized demodicosis with oral isoxazoline (off-label use may require informed consent) or injectable moxidectin.
Anemia (low PCV) noted, likely secondary to heavy hookworm or flea burden.
Prescribe ponazuril for Coccidia treatment.
My puppy has diarrhea and the vet found Coccidia in his stool sample.
Use moxidectin (Advantage Multi, ProHeart) for heartworm, fleas, roundworms, hookworms, whipworms, sarcoptic mange, ear mites (depending on formulation/species).
Ferret has dark, tarry stool, could parasites cause this?
Malodorous, purulent discharge from AD (right ear). Culture and sensitivity submitted due to chronicity and rod bacteria on cytology.
Cat's ear canal looks extremely narrow and difficult to see into.
My ferret seems lethargic because of the ear issue.
Ferret has waxy debris, possibly ear mites.
Cat has scratched the fur off behind and below her ears.
Evaluate for concurrent dermatological conditions (e.g., FAD, atopy).
Differentials for vestibular signs: Peripheral (otitis interna, idiopathic vestibular condition) vs. Central (brainstem lesion).
The discharge from my dog's ear looks slightly bloody.
Discuss prognosis for chronic ear condition.
Consider placement of BNT (Bactericidal, Nematicidal, Triamcinolone) packing in stenotic canals.
My dog yelps when other dogs play near his head.
Rabbit has pus draining from one ear.
My dog shakes his head after waking up.
Treat Otodectes with topical acaricide (e.g., Acarexx) or systemic isoxazoline. Ensure all contact animals treated.
Discuss importance of addressing primary factors (allergies, conformation) for long-term control.
Note patient breed and conformational predisposition (e.g., stenotic canals in Shar Peis, pendulous pinnae in Spaniels).
Recommend advanced imaging (CT scan) to evaluate extent of middle/inner ear involvement and rule out neoplasia.
Culture result: Pseudomonas aeruginosa sensitive only to amikacin and ticarcillin. Compounded topical therapy needed.
My puppy is a breed with heavy, floppy ears that don't get good airflow.
Cat's face looks droopy on one side, started with the ear trouble.
My cat is holding her head tilted to one side.
Provided detailed post-operative care instructions following TECA-BO surgery.
Ear infection with crusting; prescribe topical steroids.
My dog has recurring ear problems, especially after swimming.
Rabbit has crusting and hair loss around the ear base.
Discuss potential risks of TECA surgery including facial nerve damage, Horner's syndrome, abscess formation.
Pet’s ears are red and have a musty odor.
Cat seems deaf in one ear.
Chronic changes noted in ear canal: hyperplasia, stenosis, calcification.
Assess hearing crudely via response to auditory stimuli (clap test). BAER testing definitive.
Bacterial otitis; clean and start enrofloxacin.
If culture shows resistant bacteria, consider compounded topical formulations or systemic therapy based on MIC.
The skin inside my dog's ears looks red and inflamed.
My dog has environmental allergies (pollens, dust mites) causing ear issues.
Recommend ear drying solution after swimming or bathing for predisposed dogs.
Large, fluctuant aural hematoma on concave surface of right pinna. Surgical correction recommended.
Cat’s ears have a brown discharge and bad smell.
Otitis media/interna common sequela to respiratory infection in rabbits.
Document appearance of ear canal and tympanic membrane.
Advise owner to seek immediate attention if neurological signs develop or worsen.
Rabbit seems to have lost its balance, stumbling.
Tympanic membrane appears ruptured on otoscopic exam. Avoid potentially ototoxic cleaners/medications.
Otitis with waxy debris; flush and medicate.
Document size and location of aural hematoma.
Document response to previous treatments.
Consider referral to surgeon for TECA or polyp removal.
Lateral ear resection considered as palliative procedure for canal stenosis.
Use ear wick or packing with medication for severe stenosis.
Demonstrate proper ear cleaning technique and medication instillation to owner.
My cat's hearing seems diminished.
My dog had ear canal ablation surgery due to end-stage condition.
Rabbit is moving very little, seems painful.
My dog shakes his head mostly right after he gets up from sleeping.
Discuss underlying causes (allergies, conformation, moisture) and long-term management.
Systemic antifungals (e.g., ketoconazole, itraconazole) may be needed for resistant Malassezia.
Educate owner on proper ear medication administration technique.
Consider video otoscopy for deep cleaning, biopsy, or polyp removal.
Rabbit is reluctant to move.
Implement long-term maintenance protocol involving weekly cleaning and application of topical steroid.
End-stage otitis; recommend total ear canal ablation and bulla osteotomy (TECA-BO).
Cat has scratches and scabs around her ears from scratching.
Dog has hearing loss in one ear due to chronic, untreated infections.
Noticed some creamy, yellow discharge draining from my puppy's ear.
Ruptured tympanic membrane suspected; avoid ototoxic medications.
Dog has swimmer's ear.
Dog’s ears are tender and smell like yeast.
Consider referral to neurologist if central vestibular signs suspected.
Instill long-acting ear medication (e.g., Claro, Osurnia) in clinic.
My cat seems dizzy, stumbling and walking in circles.
My puppy seems unsteady on his feet, like the room is spinning.
My puppy has floppy ears that don't get much air circulation.
Rule out trauma as cause of aural hematoma.
Cat developed sudden loss of balance and circling.
Dog's ear infection has a particularly nasty, 'rotten' smell.
Otitis externa with erythema; prescribe steroid therapy.
Dog has thickened, rough skin inside the ear canal (cobblestone appearance).
My rabbit developed an abscess below its ear.
Dog's ear canal bleeds easily when cleaned.
